# Alpha101 subset — the canonical "101 Formulaic Alphas" (Kakushadze 2016,
# arXiv:1601.00991) reproducible in the atx-engine DSL after Sprint S3.
#
# Reference semantics: yli188/worldquant_alpha101 conventions (research §1.2).
# Each line below is `#<id>: <DSL expression>`. The widened-op conformance test
# (alpha_widened_conformance_test.cpp) parses, type-checks, evaluates each on a
# datafields panel (close/open/high/low/volume + derived vwap/dollar_volume/adv20
# + IndClass.sector) and asserts finite in-universe output AND oracle == VM.
#
# Fields shipped: open high low close volume vwap dollar_volume adv20
#                 IndClass.sector. Derived via alpha/datafields.hpp (S3.3).

#101: (close - open) / ((high - low) + 0.001)
#41 : power(high * low, 0.5) - vwap
#54 : (-1 * ((low - close) * power(open, 5))) / ((low - high) * power(close, 5))
#12 : sign(delta(volume, 1)) * (-1 * delta(close, 1))
#6  : -1 * correlation(open, volume, 10)
#33 : rank(-1 * (1 - (open / close)))
#13 : -1 * rank(covariance(rank(close), rank(volume), 5))
#45 : -1 * correlation(rank(close), rank(volume), 6)
#ts_max : rank(ts_max(close, 10) - close)
#scale  : scale(rank(close) - rank(open), 1)
#adv20  : rank(close / adv20)
#9  : ts_rank(volume, 5) * (-1 * ts_rank(high - close, 5))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Not-yet-expressible remainder (recorded residual — blocking dependency):
#
#  * Alphas referencing a daily `returns` field (e.g. #1, #3-style stddev(returns,
#    n)): S3.3's datafields derive vwap/dollar_volume/adv{d} but NOT daily returns
#    (returns = close/delay(close,1) - 1 is expressible inline, but the canonical
#    forms assume a precomputed `returns` column). Deferred: a `returns` datafield.
#  * Sign-conditional / ternary forms gated on returns (#1: ((returns < 0) ?
#    stddev(returns,20) : close)) — expressible once `returns` lands; the ternary
#    itself is supported (Select).
#  * `cap` (market capitalization) and `adv{d}` for d != 20 (#56, alphas needing
#    adv30/adv40/...) — adv{d} is a parameterized datafield (S3.3) but only adv20
#    is materialized on this fixture panel; deriving the needed d is mechanical.
#  * IndClass.industry / IndClass.subindustry neutralization variants — the group
#    classifiers exist (Group dtype) but this fixture panel materializes only
#    IndClass.sector.
#
# The blocking dependency for the bulk of the remainder is a `returns` datafield;
# everything else is fixture-panel field materialization, not a missing operator.
